blocks.body: Present Perfect Continuous: Forms and Use — have / has been + V-ing for process, duration up to now, and recent activity with a visible result.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In Present Perfect Continuous, we use have / has been + V-ing when we want to show an action that started earlier and continues up to now or has just finished with a visible result.","rules":[{"label":"have / has been + V-ing","color":"emerald","text":"This is the basic affirmative model of the tense.","example":"I have been working. / She has been waiting."},{"label":"Started before now","color":"blue","text":"The tense works well when an action started in the past and has not finished by the present moment.","example":"We have been living here since 2022. / He has been studying for two hours."},{"label":"Focus on process","color":"amber","text":"The focus is often on the process or duration, not only on the finished result.","example":"I have been reading all morning. / They have been talking lately."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Remember the form for each group of subjects.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"have been + V-ing","subtitle":"I have been working. / They have been waiting."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"has been + V-ing","subtitle":"She has been studying. / It has been raining."},{"label":"Spelling","title":"work → working, live → living, run → running","subtitle":"After been, use the verb with -ing."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"process / duration / recent activity","subtitle":"The action continues up to now or has just finished with a visible result."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Present Perfect Continuous","sections":[{"label":"Action still continuing","color":"emerald","description":"The action started in the past and is still continuing now.","examples":[{"en":"I have been learning English for six months.","ua":"The activity is still continuing."},{"en":"She has been waiting since 8 a.m.","ua":"The action started earlier and is still in progress."}]},{"label":"Duration up to now","color":"blue","description":"The tense highlights how long the process has continued.","examples":[{"en":"We have been working all morning.","ua":"The duration matters."},{"en":"He has been driving for three hours.","ua":"The process has lasted for a period of time."}]},{"label":"Recent activity with a visible result","color":"amber","description":"The action may have just finished, but the result is visible now.","examples":[{"en":"She is tired because she has been cleaning.","ua":"A recent process explains the present result."},{"en":"Your hands are dirty. Have you been gardening?","ua":"We can see the result now."}]},{"label":"Unfinished period","color":"violet","description":"The tense is often used with today or this week when the period has not finished yet.","examples":[{"en":"I have been reading this article today.","ua":"Today is still continuing."},{"en":"They have been meeting a lot this week.","ua":"This week is not finished yet."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick form check","intro":"See how have / has works with been and V-ing.","rows":[{"en":"I have been studying for two hours.","ua":"have been studying","note":"I + have been + V-ing"},{"en":"She has been working since morning.","ua":"has been working","note":"she + has been + V-ing"},{"en":"We have been waiting all day.","ua":"have been waiting","note":"we + have been + V-ing"},{"en":"He has been running lately.","ua":"has been running","note":"he + has been + V-ing"}],"warning":"Do not say She has been work or I have studying."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Leaving out been in the tense formula.","wrong":"❌ I have working all day.","right":"✅ I have been working all day."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using V1 or V3 instead of V-ing.","wrong":"❌ She has been cleaned the room.","right":"✅ She has been cleaning the room."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using this tense with stative verbs when another tense is needed.","wrong":"❌ I have been knowing him for years.","right":"✅ I have known him for years."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Present Perfect Continuous = have / has been + V-ing.","Use have been with I / you / we / they.","Use has been with he / she / it.","This tense highlights process, duration, or recent activity with a visible result.","After been, use the verb with -ing."]}

{"title":"Present Perfect Continuous lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-forms","current":true},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-negatives","current":false},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-questions","current":false},{"label":"Time Expressions","url":"/en/theory/present-perfect-continuous/present-perfect-continuous-time-expressions","current":false}]}
